title: Cosmos ASCII-Cam — see yourself rendered live in flowing glowing ASCII glyphs
summary: Reacts to the WEBCAM live: each frame is mirrored (selfie), downsampled to a 64x40 {brightness,motion} grid and frame-diffed, and every cell is drawn as a glowing Cosmos-tinted ASCII glyph chosen by brightness (dark cells become spaces and dots, bright cells become @ # W) so you literally see yourself flowing in characters; cells that just moved flare brighter and whiter. It gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal (a fake waving silhouette of moving Gaussian blobs) when the camera is unavailable or denied, and ships a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero dependencies. Privacy: all processing happens in-browser, nothing is uploaded.
usage_notes: RUN: open and click "enable camera", then move in frame — your silhouette resolves into a field of glowing glyphs, denser and whiter where you move. Without a camera it animates from a built-in synthetic waving silhouette so it is never blank. HOW IT REACTS: brightness picks the glyph from a dark-to-light ramp and sets glow/alpha; motion (abs frame-diff) pushes the glyph color toward white-hot and boosts its glow. DESKTOP: works the same with any webcam; no camera needed for the synthetic demo. KEY TECHNIQUES: getUserMedia({facingMode:'user'}) -> hidden <video> -> drawImage mirrored to a 64x40 offscreen canvas -> getImageData -> per-cell luminance + abs frame-diff = SIG; one render(SIG,t) maps each cell's brightness to a ramp glyph, tints by column band, and draws it with motion-driven shadow glow; synthSig(u) sums 3 looping Gaussian blobs into the same grid and diffs vs the previous frame for synthetic motion, deterministic and seamless. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: GW/GH (glyph grid res), RAMP characters, motion gain, glow blur, palette bands.
